body.title: Build acurite-capture.py — rtl_433 → CSV append + WU upload + discover mode
body.result: acurite-capture.py built: rtl_433→CSV+WU, discover mode, all rtl_433 field variants handled, stdlib only
body.intention: I intend that acurite-capture.py captures AcuRite sensor data via rtl_433, appends rows to weather.csv, uploads to WU, and has a --discover mode that prints all visible sensor IDs.
body.description:
Single Python script, stdlib only. Reads config.ini for write_path, sensor map (id→name), WU credentials, rtl_433 protocols. Runs rtl_433 as subprocess, reads JSON stdout line by line.

--discover mode: listen 5 minutes, print all AcuRite packets seen with sensor ID and model — lets user find their IDs without digging in the AcuRite app.

Daemon mode: filter by whitelisted sensor IDs (or capture all if [sensors] section is empty), parse fields (temp_F or temp_C→F, humidity, wind_mph or km/h→mph, wind_dir_deg, rain_mm→in), append CSV row, spawn background thread for WU upload. Auto-restarts rtl_433 subprocess on error.

CSV fields: timestamp,sensor_id,sensor_name,model,temp_f,humidity_pct,wind_mph,wind_dir_deg,rain_in,battery_ok

**Affected files:** acurite-free/acurite-capture.py
**Design rules:** stdlib only (subprocess, csv, json, urllib, configparser, threading, argparse); no SQLite; no Flask
**Scope boundary:** IN — capture, CSV write, WU upload, discover mode. OUT — web UI, cloud API calls (cloud sync is OS-level).
**Completion criteria:** --discover prints sensor IDs from a live rtl_433 stream; daemon mode appends rows to weather.csv; WU upload fires on each valid packet when enabled.
